---
title: "Vignette Title"
date: "YYYY-MM-DD"
date-modified: "YYYY-MM-DD"
version: "1.0"
description: "One-sentence summary of what this vignette covers."
categories: [category1, category2]
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: false  # set to true if vignette is prose-heavy with long code blocks
execute:
  eval: false
  echo: true
---

# Overview

<!--
One to three sentences describing:
- What this vignette covers
- Who it is for or when to use it
- Any cross-references to related vignettes

Example:
  This vignette covers X. For background on Y, see `vignettes/y.qmd`.
-->

---

# Section Title

<!--
GUIDANCE FOR WRITING SECTIONS:

Content type — pick the style that fits:

1. CONCEPTUAL / REFERENCE (explains a concept, decision rule, or set of guidelines)
   - Use prose paragraphs
   - Use tables for decision rules or comparisons
   - Use callout blocks for warnings, tips, or important constraints
   - Avoid code unless a short snippet genuinely aids understanding

2. HOW-TO / PROCEDURAL (walks through steps to accomplish a task)
   - Use numbered steps for sequential procedures
   - Use code blocks for commands or scripts
   - Keep code blocks focused — one logical action per block
   - Add a brief prose line before each code block explaining what it does

3. REFERENCE CARD / QUICK REFERENCE (summary for scanning, not reading)
   - Use a table or a minimal code block
   - Place at the end of the vignette

HEADING LEVELS:
  # H1   — top-level sections (Overview, major topics)
  ## H2  — subsections within a topic
  ### H3 — sub-subsections; use sparingly

CALLOUT TYPES:
  ::: callout-note      — supplementary information, tips
  ::: callout-important — constraints, limitations, things that will break
  ::: callout-warning   — actions that could cause data loss or hard-to-fix problems
  :::

CROSS-REFERENCES:
  - Other vignettes:     `vignettes/other-vignette.qmd`
  - Conversation files:  `conversations/template-name.qmd`
  - Prompt files:        `prompts/prompt-name.md`
  - External docs:       [Link text](https://url)

CODE BLOCKS:
  - Python executable:   ```{python}
  - R executable:        ```{r}
  - Bash (display only): ```bash
  - All non-executable blocks should be display-only (no {})
-->

---

# Section Title

---

# Quick Reference

<!--
Optional. Include if the vignette covers enough discrete commands or rules
that a summary table or code block would be useful for scanning later.
-->
